behaviors.description: Reject webhook when `X-Signature-256` does not match raw body; do not forward event.
behaviors.justification: A pure unit test of HMAC comparison can miss real failures in handler wiring (raw-body extraction, header parsing, and forwarding side effect). The failure boundary is the HTTP handler plus queue integration.
behaviors.test_description: Run the webhook route with real request parsing configured for raw bytes. Send a POST with an intentionally bad signature and valid-looking payload. Assert HTTP 401, and assert no queue record/write occurred.

behaviors.description: Persist event to queue before returning HTTP 200.
behaviors.justification: This is an ordering guarantee across handler and persistent queue modules. Mocking queue writes can hide real ordering/flush bugs; use real local persistence.
behaviors.test_description: Use the real queue implementation (e.g., local DB/file-backed queue). Inject a controllable delay/trace point around response send. Verify queue write commit is observable before 200 is returned.

behaviors.description: Return HTTP response without waiting for downstream processing completion.
behaviors.justification: The key failure is async control-flow coupling between enqueue/ack and downstream worker invocation. This is cross-module behavior but does not require OS/network beyond local app wiring.
behaviors.test_description: Configure downstream processor to block (e.g., promise resolves after long delay). Send valid webhook and assert response returns quickly after enqueue, while processor completion happens later.

behaviors.description: Response arrives within 3 seconds wall-clock even under slow concurrent downstream work.
behaviors.justification: This is a real-time SLA under concurrency and event-loop pressure. In-process mocks/fake timers can miss scheduler/OS/socket behavior and true latency regressions.
behaviors.test_description: Start the service in a real runtime process, generate concurrent webhook traffic while earlier events trigger intentionally slow downstream processing, and measure end-to-end HTTP latency. Assert each acknowledgment is under 3 seconds wall-clock.

behaviors.description: Do not enqueue duplicate event IDs.
behaviors.justification: Idempotency depends on persistence semantics (unique key/check-then-insert race behavior). Unit tests can miss real duplicate acceptance due to storage-level behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Using the real persistent queue store, submit the same event ID twice (including near-concurrent submissions). Assert only one queue entry exists and second attempt is treated as duplicate.

behaviors.description: When queue is full, return 503 with `Retry-After`.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on actual queue-capacity signal propagation to HTTP mapping. Pure mocks can hide mismatches between queue error types and handler response behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Fill the real queue to configured capacity, then send one more valid webhook. Assert HTTP 503 and presence/value format of `Retry-After`; assert overflow event is not persisted.

behaviors.description: Persistent queue enforces configured capacity and rejects overflow.
behaviors.justification: This is primarily queue-module behavior with real local persistence/DB constraints. Requires actual storage interactions, but not external OS/network processes.
behaviors.test_description: Instantiate queue with a small explicit capacity using its real storage backend. Enqueue up to limit successfully, then enqueue one extra and assert rejection error/state with no persisted overflow item.
